message:
chore(beads): review-pass-2 — 1 CRITICAL + 2 HIGH conformance beads filed

Deeper /testing-conformance-harnesses pass focused on the
fnp-* vs upstream-pandas oracle pipeline. Workspace baseline
remains green (3170 passed / 0 failed / 13 ignored) — all six
row-MI epic children landed (1zzp.1..6 closed, umbrella
reaches its all-children-done state).

  frankenpandas-d6xa [CRITICAL] conformance: 89 live_oracle_*
                                tests silently skip in CI — the
                                core pandas-parity claim is not
                                enforced by ci.yml. legacy_pandas_code/
                                is gitignored, allow_system_pandas_fallback
                                defaults to false, ci.yml installs
                                no pandas. Every live_oracle_* test
                                matches on HarnessError::OracleUnavailable
                                and early-returns. CI sees "388 passed"
                                but ~89 of those are silent skips.

  frankenpandas-boyr [HIGH]     conformance: 1249 golden fixtures
                                have no pandas-version provenance.
                                Fixtures reflect some past pandas 2.x
                                snapshot; when pandas ships a point
                                release that changes semantics our
                                frozen fixtures still claim conformance
                                against the OLD behavior. Silent drift
                                the longer it ages.

  frankenpandas-qi6y [HIGH]     conformance: no aggregate counter
                                for live-oracle ran/skipped/failed.
                                Observability half of d6xa — even
                                after fixing CI pandas install, we
                                need LiveOracleReport { ran, skipped,
                                failed } surfaced through fp-ci-gates
                                so "did we actually test against
                                pandas?" becomes a first-class metric.

All three interlock: d6xa fixes the pipeline, qi6y makes the pipeline
observable, boyr prevents the pipeline from rotting. Each bead names
concrete fix shapes (config flags to add, CI steps to add, new
counters, new fields on fixture schema).

Applied skill hooks:
- Pattern 1 Differential Testing → found live-oracle pipeline broken
- Fixture Provenance rule → found version pin missing
- "Before claiming conformance, prove it" → found observability gap

No shipped-code regression. Pure pipeline-integrity findings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Restore compile so other beads can land.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:42:07.676419104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:48:51.373528981Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:48:51.371665925Z","close_reason":"shipped 5c1450f: 4 new FixtureOperation variants (DataFrameApplySemAxis0, DataFrameApplyProdAxis1, DataFrameApplyProductAxis1, DataFrameApplyNuniqueAxis0) wired into 5 exhaustive match sites (CC-005 capability gate + 2 dataframe-aggregate dispatches + 2 operation-set arms), routing through frame.apply(name, axis) canonical path. fp-conformance: compile restored, 340/12 baseline. Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-bovg","title":"Add series_rolling_sum/std/min/max edge case coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:27:27.905346719Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T20:28:12.065585916Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T20:28:12.063822058Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-boyr","title":"[HIGH] conformance: 1249 golden fixtures have no pandas-version provenance — silent drift against pandas releases","description":"Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses skill's Fixture Provenance rule + the "fixtures can become stale" concern.

**Observation.** crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets/ contains 1249 JSON fixture files. Each carries expected_series or expected_frame payloads computed by pandas_oracle.py at some generation point. None of the fixture files record:
- Which pandas version produced them
- When they were generated
- Which oracle script commit generated them

The fixtures/README.md describes the generation workflow but the Reference line says "pandas (system install, typically 2.x)" — no version pin. .github/workflows/ci.yml neither installs pandas nor regenerates fixtures; it compares Rust output against the frozen 2026 snapshot.

**Concrete risk.** Pandas 2.3.0, 3.0.0, etc. will land. When a pandas point release changes a semantic (they do — see https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/whatsnew/ changelog), our 1249 fixtures keep reflecting the OLD behavior. Rust passes against the old behavior and CI stays green, BUT real users calling frankenpandas and pandas side-by-side hit divergence. The longer the fixtures age without regeneration, the wider the drift.

**Severity.** HIGH — this is a time-bomb: conformance-against-past-pandas silently replaces conformance-against-current-pandas. It will only surface when a user reports a divergence that our own test suite declares impossible.

**Fix shape:**
1. Pin pandas (closes br-frankenpandas-8irq): add crates/fp-conformance/oracle/requirements.txt with pandas==X.Y.Z. CI installs exactly this version.
2. Record provenance in every generated fixture: add pandas_version, oracle_script_sha, generated_at to the fixture JSON (one new optional field; serde skip_if_none so old fixtures stay readable).
3. Stale-fixture detector: new scripts/check_fixture_freshness.sh compares fixture pandas_version field to oracle/requirements.txt pin. Fails CI on mismatch.
4. Quarterly regeneration ritual: document in AGENTS.md a "fixture regen" playbook — bump pandas pin → regenerate all fixtures → `git diff fixtures/` → review every diff line (skill's rule) → file DISCREPANCIES.md entries for any intentional divergences → commit atomically. First regen after this bead lands.
5. Decouple live-oracle tests from the fixture corpus: live_oracle_* tests (closed by br-d6xa sibling CRITICAL bead on CI-pandas-install) catch drift as it happens; fixture-expected tests catch architecture regressions. Both layers needed.

Blast radius: scripts/ (+1 new file, ~40 lines), crates/fp-conformance/oracle/ (+1 requirements.txt), AGENTS.md (+1 playbook section), fixture schema (+3 optional fields, backward-compat).

Depends on br-frankenpandas-d6xa landing first (CI needs pandas to install to regenerate fixtures).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:11:20.990363375Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T19:11:20.990363375Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","drift","provenance","review"]}

Refactored apply_parse_date_combinations to share apply_one_parse_date_combination core. Clippy clean; 190 fp-io tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-d4ht","title":"Add dataframe head/tail saturating negative-n strict conformance packets","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T00:28:04.982027231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T00:32:11.427446798Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T00:32:11.425690144Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-d6xa","title":"[CRITICAL] conformance: 89 live_oracle_* tests silently skip in CI — core pandas-parity claim is not enforced","description":"Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses skill's Pattern 1 (Differential Testing) + the "Before claiming conformance, prove it" rule.

**Observation.** 89 live_oracle_*_matches_pandas tests exist in crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs. Each one dispatches through capture_live_oracle_expected → subprocess pandas_oracle.py. The default HarnessConfig sets:

  oracle_root: repo_root.join("legacy_pandas_code/pandas")
  allow_system_pandas_fallback: false

When oracle_root.exists() is false, capture_live_oracle_expected returns HarnessError::OracleUnavailable. The 89 tests match on that variant and early-return with an eprintln!("live pandas unavailable; skipping ..."). No test fails. No test is marked ignored. No summary counter is emitted.

**The repo intentionally gitignores legacy_pandas_code/** (confirmed: .gitignore line "legacy_pandas_code/"). .github/workflows/ci.yml does NOT install pandas, does NOT set FP_PYTHON_BIN, does NOT checkout a pandas oracle source tree, does NOT set allow_system_pandas_fallback=true.

**Consequence.** In CI as shipped today, all 89 live_oracle_* tests silently pass by skipping. The entire differential conformance layer — the exact thing the README markets as "differential conformance tests verify against the actual pandas oracle" — is a no-op in CI. The only thing CI actually verifies is the 1249 pre-generated packet fixtures (FixtureExpected mode, golden-file comparison), which themselves were generated by some earlier pandas version that is not pinned anywhere (br-frankenpandas-8irq covers the version pin separately).

**Severity.** CRITICAL — the core product claim (pandas parity, verified by oracle) is not actually enforced by CI. Any regression that only the live oracle would catch (e.g. a new pandas point release changing semantics we match; a Rust-side bug that golden fixtures predate) lands on main silently.

**Fix shape:**
1. Count-and-fail: HarnessConfig gains a require_live_oracle: bool, default false. When true, OracleUnavailable converts to test failure instead of early-return. ci.yml sets the env var that flips this on.
2. CI installs pandas: ci.yml adds a Python setup step + pip install pandas==PINNED_VERSION (coordinates with br-frankenpandas-8irq). Then clones / mirrors legacy_pandas_code or points oracle_root at the pip-installed pandas via allow_system_pandas_fallback=true.
3. Skip counter: LiveOracleReport aggregates per-test skipped/run/failed counts, printed at end of lib-test run. CI asserts skipped_count == 0 or an explicit allowlist.
4. README update: add "When live pandas is unavailable, these 89 tests skip silently — not a gap in the oracle, a gap in the env" disclosure until (1)-(3) land.

Blast radius: ci.yml (+ ~12 lines), fp-conformance HarnessConfig + tests (+ ~60 lines for the counter + flag). No code-logic change.

Parallel bead: br-frankenpandas-8irq (pin pandas version) becomes a prerequisite for this bead — landing live-oracle-in-CI without a pinned pandas causes CI flakiness on any pandas update.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:10:40.629610903Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T19:10:40.629610903Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","conformance","critical","oracle","review"]}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-qfah","title":"pandas: DataFrame.to_markdown tablefmt parity beyond github/pipe/grid/plain","description":"py signature:
df.to_markdown(tablefmt="simple")
df.to_markdown(tablefmt="psql")
df.to_markdown(tablefmt="rst")

Current fp-frame to_markdown in crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs only supports github/pipe/grid/plain and CompatibilityRejects other tablefmt values. Add parity for common tabulate-style formats that pandas accepts, with snapshot-style tests for each renderer.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:56:14.443691441Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:33:28.510160520Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:33:28.508150797Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-qgxb","title":"fp-frame Series.empty property parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:29:20.206252611Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T09:34:57.783673490Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T09:34:57.781582681Z","close_reason":"Implemented Series::empty in commit 141b6bf, 2 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-qi6y","title":"[HIGH] conformance: no aggregate counter for live-oracle ran/skipped/failed — silent-skip isn't observable","description":"Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses skill's Pattern 1 (Differential Testing) observations, combined with a fresh count.

**Observation.** grep for test function names of the form `fn live_oracle_.*_matches_pandas` in crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs returns 89 tests. These tests cover:
- DataFrameGroupBy.rolling.mean/sum/min/max/count/std/var (7 tests from br-1zzp family)
- DataFrameGroupBy.resample min/max/count/first/last/sum/mean (7 tests)
- DataFrame.apply sem_axis0/axis1 + alias variants
- Series.dt.* accessor chain
- Merge ordered (3 tests)
- Row MultiIndex construction / indexing / reshape (newly added in d35ab20 for br-1zzp.5)
- ... and ~65 more

**Gap.** `cargo test -p fp-conformance` output prints 388 passed without distinguishing how many of those 388 were FixtureExpected packets (compare against frozen JSON) vs how many were LiveLegacyPandas dispatch (compare against live pandas call). A human reviewer cannot answer "how much of our pandas parity did CI actually verify?" from the test output alone.

There's no aggregation test that asserts "at least N live_oracle_* tests ran successfully against the live oracle," only individual tests that skip silently when oracle is absent. This means:
- A CI where legacy_pandas_code is missing: 89 silent skips, test count still passes — false-green.
- A CI where a pandas upgrade breaks the oracle script: every test skips with OracleUnavailable, no one notices.
- Local-only developers: tests run; but there's no committed metric confirming "this commit's oracle run matched pandas."

**Severity.** HIGH — this is the observability half of br-frankenpandas-d6xa (CRITICAL sibling on CI-pandas-install). Even after fixing the install, we need to surface "did we actually test against pandas?" as a first-class counter.

**Fix shape:**
1. Add LiveOracleReport { ran: usize, skipped: usize, failed: usize } aggregated across all live_oracle_* tests via a lazy_static registry (each test bumps a counter on ok/skip/fail).
2. New e2e-level test that asserts report.skipped == 0 when oracle_root is present, and (once d6xa lands) fails loudly when both oracle_root is absent AND require_live_oracle=true.
3. `cargo run -p fp-conformance --bin fp-ci-gates` (the existing CI binary at ci.yml:line-CI-gate-topology) gets a new gate that emits the report to artifacts/ci/live_oracle_report.json. Governance gate (scripts/governance_gate_check.sh) reads that and fails when ran < 89.
4. Surface in README: "N live-oracle tests ran · M skipped · K failed" near the Conformance capability row.

Closes the observability loop so "we silently skipped 89 tests" becomes a loud failure instead of a silent passing build.

Depends on br-frankenpandas-d6xa — without live oracle actually running in CI, this report would just always say "ran: 0, skipped: 89" and CI would fail. They should land together.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:11:56.845078711Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T19:11:56.845078711Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","conformance","observability","review"]}
